name: Daube De Queue De Boeuf
recipeIngredient: 1 head of garlic, cloves peeled and halved
500 g carrots, peeled into 2 cm (3/4 in) slices
2.25 liters red wine (such as Cote de Provence)
2.5 kg oxtail, cut into 10 cm (4 in) pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Oxtail is one of the most economical and flavorful cuts of meat, ideal for marinating. Marinade it with red wine, herbs, and vegetables for at least 3 days, or longer for deeper flavor.
2. Before serving, allow the stew to cool completely so all fat can be skimmed off.
3. Serve this rich stew over thick noodles in warmed soup bowls, accompanied by a tossed salad and a robust red wine.
4. In a large nonreactive casserole, mix all marinade ingredients through peppercorns. Add oxtail pieces and marinate covered in the refrigerator for up to 5 days, stirring occasionally.
5. The day before serving, remove the casserole from the refrigerator. Remove oxtail pieces, drain well, and return them with salt pork to the marinade. Cover and simmer over medium heat until meat is tender, about 2 to 2.5 hours. Keep the mixture bubbling gently; skim any impurities or grease.
6. Cool completely, refrigerate overnight.
7. The next day, remove and discard solidified fat. Add fresh carrots, salt, and pepper, and simmer until heated through, checking seasoning as needed.
8. To serve, bring a large pot of water to boil with salt. Cook pasta just until firm, then drain well. Divide pasta among shallow soup bowls. Carefully remove oxtail pieces, place on carving board, and cut into chunks. Spoon sauce and vegetables over meat, sprinkle coarse salt, and serve immediately.
